human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for gaming-specific boundary discipline, self-exclusion and responsible-gaming controls, player-account and cash evidence handling, patron-dispute routing, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to casinos, gaming, and wagering player-service intake, player-account and wallet servicing, self-exclusion-safe communication, patron dispute escalation, and evidence-backed routing tied to player, wager, ticket, cage, and complaint records.

Authority sources cover New Jersey gaming regulations for internet gaming, sports wagering, and self-exclusion; Nevada patron-dispute and interactive-gaming control standards; FinCEN casino AML guidance; and the AGA Responsible Gaming Code of Conduct.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported exclusion and cash exceptions, AML-sensitive handoff, settled-wager routing, repeat failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/casinos-gaming-and-wagering/player-services-specialist/ so player services stays separate from sportsbook settlement, cage, AML, surveillance, compliance, and responsible-gaming decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around self-exclusion, wager adjudication, cash exceptions, suspicious-activity communication, and legal or compliance interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated player services specialist that supports evidence-backed player-service handling and policy-aware escalation for casinos, gaming, and wagering operators.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
